function_explanation: Performs matrix multiplication between a real input tensor and a complex weight matrix, returning the real and imaginary components of the result stacked along a new dimension.
function_docstring:
Args:
    x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (..., K), typically (Batch, In_Features).
    F (torch.Tensor): Complex weight matrix of shape (K, N, 2), where the last dimension represents the real and imaginary parts (index 0 for real, 1 for imaginary).

Returns:
    torch.Tensor: A tensor containing the real and imaginary parts of the matrix multiplication. If input is (B, K), output is (B, 2, N). If input is (K), output is (2, N). The real and imaginary results are stacked along the second-to-last dimension.
usage_example:
import torch

# Define input dimensions
B, K, N = 10, 5, 3

# Create random input tensor (real)
x = torch.randn(B, K)

# Create random complex weight matrix (K, N, 2)
# F[..., 0] is real part, F[..., 1] is imaginary part
F = torch.randn(K, N, 2)

# Perform complex matmul
output = torch_complex_matmul(x, F)

# Output shape will be (B, 2, N)
print(f"Input shape: {x.shape}")
print(f"Weight shape: {F.shape}")
print(f"Output shape: {output.shape}")
